stemHtml:
Refer to figure or Performance Manual MRJT 1 Figure 4.24.
With regard to the drift down performance of the twin jet aeroplane, what is meant by "equivalent gross weight at engine failure"?

options.a: This gross weight accounts for the lower Mach number at higher temperatures.
options.b: The equivalent gross weight at engine failure is the actual gross weight corrected for OAT higher than ISA +10° C.
options.c: The increment accounts for the higher fuel flow at higher temperatures.
options.d: The increment represents fuel used before engine failure.
